title: Kids With the Greatest Number of Candies

testcases.input: candies = [2,3,5,1,3], extraCandies = 3 Output: [true,true,true,false,true] Explanation: If you give all extraCandies to: - Kid 1, they will have 2 + 3 = 5 candies, which is the greatest among the kids. - Kid 2, they will have 3 + 3 = 6 candies, which is the greatest among the kids. - Kid 3, they will have 5 + 3 = 8 candies, which is the greatest among the kids. - Kid 4, they will have 1 + 3 = 4 candies, which is not the greatest among the kids. - Kid 5, they will have 3 + 3 = 6 candies, which is the greatest among the kids.
testcases.expectedOutput: [true,true,true,false,true] Explanation: If you give all extraCandies to: - Kid 1, they will have 2 + 3 = 5 candies, which is the greatest among the kids. - Kid 2, they will have 3 + 3 = 6 candies, which is the greatest among the kids. - Kid 3, they will have 5 + 3 = 8 candies, which is the greatest among the kids. - Kid 4, they will have 1 + 3 = 4 candies, which is not the greatest among the kids. - Kid 5, they will have 3 + 3 = 6 candies, which is the greatest among the kids.

testcases.input: candies = [4,2,1,1,2], extraCandies = 1 Output: [true,false,false,false,false] Explanation: There is only 1 extra candy. Kid 1 will always have the greatest number of candies, even if a different kid is given the extra candy.
